description_long: This object is a large, multi-barreled shotgun with a distinctive rotary design, reminiscent of a Gatling or minigun style. The main body is predominantly black with red accents, including three visible barrel rings and a few highlighted details. The shotgun features six parallel barrels, each reinforced by three red bands, and a cylindrical housing at the rear, which likely contains the firing mechanism. There is a prominent handle at the back for carrying or aiming, and the side of the housing displays white geometric symbols. The overall construction appears robust and industrial, with a focus on durability and high-capacity firing. The design suggests it is intended for heavy-duty use in urban environments, possibly for crowd control or defense.
description: A large, black rotary shotgun with six barrels, red accent rings, and a robust industrial design featuring a rear handle and geometric markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the six-barrel rotary configuration, rear handle, and white geometric symbols on the main housing.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular arrangement of the six barrels and the red accent rings encircling them.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same six-barrel structure, handle, and cylindrical housing, with fewer visible markings.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the cylindrical housing and the handle, with minimal detail on the barrels.
attribute_barrel_configuration: rotary multi-barrel configuration with six parallel barrels, reinforced by three red rings
attribute_trigger_guard_design: Not visibly present; trigger area appears integrated into the main housing without a distinct guard
